title: API Hardening: Input Validation & 404 Error Handling
description: Improve the FastAPI task management API by replacing untyped dict parameters with the Task Pydantic model and adding proper 404 error handling for missing tasks.

epics.title: Input Validation via Pydantic Models
epics.description: Replace raw dict parameters in task endpoints with typed Pydantic models to enforce field constraints and prevent mass-assignment vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Enhance Task model with field constraints
stories.description: Add Field() constraints to the existing Task Pydantic model: minimum/maximum lengths for title and description, a status enum allowlist, and extra='forbid' to block unexpected keys.
acceptance_criteria: Task.title has min_length=1 and max_length=200
Task.description has max_length=1000
Task.status is constrained to the enum values: 'open', 'in_progress', 'done'
Task model uses ConfigDict(extra='forbid') to reject unexpected fields
Sending an invalid status value returns HTTP 422 with a descriptive error
Sending an extra field (e.g. 'admin': true) returns HTTP 422

tasks.title: Import Field and ConfigDict from pydantic
Add Literal['open', 'in_progress', 'done'] type annotation to Task.status

stories.title: Use Task model in create_task endpoint
stories.description: Replace the untyped `task: dict` parameter in POST /tasks/ with `task: Task` so FastAPI automatically validates the request body.
acceptance_criteria: POST /tasks/ accepts only valid Task fields
Sending a request without 'title' returns HTTP 422
Sending a title longer than 200 chars returns HTTP 422
Valid request returns the created task with id, title, description, status
The stored task preserves the validated values from the model

tasks.title: Change create_task signature from task: dict to task: Task
Use task.model_dump() to build the stored dict instead of **task

stories.title: Add TaskUpdate model and use in update_task endpoint
stories.description: Create a TaskUpdate Pydantic model with all-optional fields for partial updates, and replace the untyped `updates: dict` parameter in PUT /tasks/{task_id}.
acceptance_criteria: TaskUpdate model exists in src/models.py with optional title, description, status fields
Each optional field carries the same constraints as Task (max_length, enum, etc.)
PUT /tasks/{task_id} uses TaskUpdate instead of dict
Sending an invalid status value in an update returns HTTP 422
Sending only a subset of fields performs a partial update (other fields unchanged)
Sending extra fields returns HTTP 422

tasks.title: Define TaskUpdate(BaseModel) in models.py with Optional fields
Change update_task signature from updates: dict to updates: TaskUpdate
Use updates.model_dump(exclude_unset=True) for the partial merge

epics.title: 404 Error Handling for Missing Tasks
epics.description: All endpoints that look up a task by ID should return a structured HTTP 404 response when the task does not exist, rather than raising an unhandled KeyError.

stories.title: Return 404 in get_task when task not found
stories.description: Add an existence check and raise HTTPException(status_code=404) in the GET /tasks/{task_id} endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: GET /tasks/nonexistent returns HTTP 404
Response body is {"detail": "Task not found"}
GET /tasks/{valid_id} still returns the task correctly
No unhandled KeyError is raised for missing IDs

tasks.title: Import HTTPException from fastapi in tasks.py
Add `if task_id not in tasks: raise HTTPException(404, 'Task not found')` to get_task

stories.title: Return 404 in update_task when task not found
stories.description: Add an existence check and raise HTTPException(status_code=404) in the PUT /tasks/{task_id} endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: PUT /tasks/nonexistent returns HTTP 404
Response body is {"detail": "Task not found"}
PUT /tasks/{valid_id} with valid body still updates correctly
No unhandled KeyError is raised for missing IDs

tasks.title: Add `if task_id not in tasks: raise HTTPException(404, 'Task not found')` to update_task

stories.title: Return 404 in delete_task when task not found
stories.description: Add an existence check and raise HTTPException(status_code=404) in the DELETE /tasks/{task_id} endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: DELETE /tasks/nonexistent returns HTTP 404
Response body is {"detail": "Task not found"}
DELETE /tasks/{valid_id} still deletes and returns {"deleted": id}
No unhandled KeyError is raised for missing IDs

tasks.title: Add `if task_id not in tasks: raise HTTPException(404, 'Task not found')` to delete_task
